title: matthiola fruticulosa subsp. valesiaca
intro: Matthiola fruticulosa subsp. valesiaca, also known as Wallflower, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its fragrant, bright yellow flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an expectorant, relieving congestion and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Matthiola fruticulosa subsp. valesiaca include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve mood, and support respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
